title: Playwright Request API reference
summary: `requestfailed` fires when a request could not be fulfilled, while HTTP error status codes still count as successful network completion from Playwright's perspective. This supports treating many `ERR_ABORTED` logs as cancellation signals rather than raw HTTP failures.

title: Playwright Response API reference
summary: Playwright response bodies can be accessed as bytes or text, which supports preserving raw body data and charset-aware decoded text alongside captured responses.

title: WHATWG HTML document encoding model
summary: HTML encoding is determined from byte streams plus metadata such as BOM, transport headers, and in-document declarations. This supports preserving charset metadata instead of assuming UTF-8 during replay analysis.

title: MDN meta charset reference
summary: `<meta charset>` remains an important in-document encoding signal and should be considered after transport metadata and BOM during decode heuristics.

title: MDN @charset reference
summary: CSS can declare its encoding with `@charset`, which justifies stylesheet-specific detection during network capture and later replay processing.

title: Scrapy scheduler and request priority documentation
summary: Priority queues and crawl order controls are central to practical crawler frontier design, which supports moving away from first-seen queue insertion toward ranked frontier selection.

title: Apache Nutch scoring filter API
summary: Nutch exposes scoring hooks for new URLs and outlinks before fetch selection, reinforcing the decision to add explicit candidate scoring and selection reasons to frontier handling.

title: Google Search Central large-site crawl budget guidance
summary: Query-heavy and faceted URLs can consume crawl budget without improving representative discovery. This supports non-destructive query penalties and diversity caps in frontier selection.

title: Apollo Client fetch policy documentation for cache-and-network
summary: `cache-and-network` returns cached results immediately and still sends a network request for fresh data. This supports treating some same-site GraphQL calls as supporting refresh behavior when equivalent bootstrap state is already embedded in the page shell.

title: MDN Using Service Workers guide
summary: Service workers can intercept document and subresource requests through the fetch event and populate a Cache Storage-backed offline experience. This is useful background for deciding when replay should rely on explicit adapter routing instead of browser-controlled service worker interception.

title: MDN ServiceWorkerGlobalScope fetch event reference
summary: The service worker fetch event can respond to requests from controlled documents and their subresources. This supports the decision to keep service workers blocked during capture until replay can distinguish adapter-visible requests from service-worker-satisfied requests.

title: MDN Cache interface reference
summary: The Cache API stores request-response pairs under service worker or window control, which matters when comparing generated replay adapters with browser-managed offline caching strategies.

title: Playwright Mock APIs and HAR replay documentation
summary: Playwright can fulfill requests from mocked routes or HAR files, which provides a useful comparison point for the repository's own manifest-driven replay adapter and bounded request matching rules.

title: Playwright network interception documentation
summary: Playwright's route interception and HAR support clarify where browser-context mocking ends and where repo-specific replay adapters still add value, especially for route visibility and deterministic request classification.

title: Next.js Large Page Data error guide
summary: For the Pages Router, `__NEXT_DATA__` is the inlined JSON payload the client parses before hydration. This is direct evidence that replay should preserve and inspect inline bootstrap blobs before assuming a later runtime request is first-paint critical.

title: Next.js App Router data fetching guide
summary: App Router data fetching emphasizes server-side fetches, streamed route segments, and less reliance on a single `__NEXT_DATA__` payload. This supports using different bootstrap heuristics for modern streaming applications than for Pages Router HTML.

title: Next.js caching deep dive
summary: Next.js App Router uses request memoization and a server-side Data Cache, which means some repeated data fetches may not appear as independent browser requests. This matters when deciding whether a captured request is truly required for replay fidelity.

title: Apollo Automatic Persisted Queries documentation
summary: APQ allows clients to send operation metadata and query hashes without including the full GraphQL document text. This supports replay matching strategies that rely on operation name, variables hash, and persisted query metadata when `query` is absent.
